methods.description: Returns a matrix that transforms between the coordinate spaces of the source and target devices.

members.name: fileStartTimecode
members.description: Preloads the movie by opening it and pre-reading the first frames. Use the isFullyPreRead member to see if it's ready to play. This is done in a way to minimize impact on the existing playback of the application. If movies are being loaded on startup it's better to just call cook() on the Movie File In

members.name: metadata
members.description: Dictionary containing all the metadata.

members.name: fileHeight
members.description: Height of the movie, in pixels.

members.name: fileWidth
members.description: Width of the movie, in pixels.

members.name: hasAudio
members.description: True if the movie contains audio.

members.name: hasDecodeErrors
members.description: True if any frames failed to decode, likely due to file corruption. Currently only works on Hap codec files.

members.name: index
members.description: Current movie index.

members.name: indexFraction
members.description: Current movie index expressed as a fraction of total length.

members.name: isFullyPreRead
members.description: True if the movie has pre-read all of its Pre-Read frames and is ready to play. For single images this is true when the image is ready to be shown. When a movie is playing, this member will flip between True and False as its pre-read frames get consumed/refilled. When a movie isn't playing forward, this member can tell you if the movie is in an optimal state to start playing back from its currently selected frame/cued frame.

members.name: isInvalid
members.description: True if the movie has failed to load.

members.name: isLastFrame
members.description: True if the movie is currently showing its last frame.

members.name: isLoopFrame
members.description: True if the movie has just looped and is showing the first frame after a loop.

members.name: isOddField
members.description: True if the current displayed de-interlaced frame is the odd field.

members.name: isOpen
members.description: True if the file has been opened.

members.name: isOpening
members.description: True when the file is opening.

members.name: isPreloading
members.description: True when the file is preloading.

members.name: lastIndexUploaded
members.description: The index of the if the last frame uploaded to the

members.name: numHeaders
members.description: Returns the number of key-value pairs in the file's header.

members.name: numImages
members.description: The number of images in the movie.

members.name: numSeconds
members.description: The number of seconds in the movie.

members.name: rate
members.description: The movie sample rate.

members.name: sourceChannels
members.description: A list of the available channels in the file e.g. R, G, B, A for typical color images.

members.name: start
members.description: The start index of the movie.

members.name: trueIndex
members.description: The actual current index of the movie, disregarding trimming and other options.

members.name: trueNumImages
members.description: The actual number of images contained in the movie, not affected by trimming.

members.name: timecode
members.description: Get a Timecode object for the timecode data representation of the true current index of the movie. See

methods.name: findHeader
methods.description: Returns the value of the header with the given key. This method will return a blank string if the header is not found.

methods.name: getHeader
methods.description: Returns a tuple with the key and value of the header at the given index. This method will throw an error if the index is not valid. The numHeaders member can be used to get the number of valid headers in the file.

methods.name: unload
methods.description: Unloads the movie and frees its memory usage. The movie will open again next time it cooks, so make sure nothing is still using it to keep it closed.

methods.name: preload
methods.description: Preloads the movie by opening it and pre-reading the first frames. Use the isFullyPreRead member to see if it's ready to play. This is done in a way to minimize impact on the existing playback of the application. If movies are being loaded on startup it's better to just call cook() on the Movie File In

members.name: fileSuffix
members.description: Returns the generated file suffix. It will be generated based on the values of the parameters Unique Suffix and N, plus the file extension. It will take one of two forms:

members.name: isConnected

members.description: The cap height of the font, as described by the font's metrics. This is usually the height of a capital H.

members.description: The x height of the font, as described by the font's metrics. This is usually the height of a lower case x.

methods.name: fontSupportsChars
methods.description: Returns True if every character maps to a glyph. This doesn't mean the font supports the language in all cases. Glyphs that come from ligatures etc. may still be missing from the font.

methods.name: evalTextSize
methods.description: Returns a tuple of width, height of the given string using the operators settings. Does not include the word wrap and auto size font options.

members.name: calibrationParameters
members.description: Dictionary containing the intrinsics and extrinsic stereo parameters for unrectified/distorted images. This includes focal point, optical center, distortion, field of view, and stereo transformation matrix.

members.name: calibrationParametersRaw
members.description: Dictionary containing the intrinsics and extrinsic stereo parameters for unrectified/distorted images. This includes focal point, optical center, distortion, field of view, and stereo transformation matrix.
